observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE directs:
a. The Municipal Engineer to provide report on the cost and status of on-
going projects and submit the details of infrastructure projects as the
need arises for proper recording in the books of the on-going projects
and facilitate the transfer of these projects from the CIP accounts to
the appropriate PPE accounts upon completion;
b. The Municipal Accountant to immediately recognize completed
projects as PPE immediately upon completion and availability for use
and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses, regardless of the
timing of payment, in accordance with the accrual basis of accounting
prescribed under IPSAS 1 and the recognition criteria under IPSAS
17; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entries to correct the understatement of the appropriate/ related PPE
and overstatement of CIP accounts in order to fairly present the
affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Head of Inventory Committee
to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies held for distribution or
consumption and semi-expendable properties and ensure prompt
submission of duly accomplished RPCSP not later than July 31 and January
31 of each year for the first and second semesters respectively in order to
ascertain the condition and completeness of the inventory accounts, hence
ensure reliable FS at year-end.

We recommended the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Coordinate with the BAC Chairman to determine possible payment
of the bid security posted in the form of cash by securing copies of said
security/ Official Receipt together with the signed contract which will
be the basis in the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher;
b. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer for the issuance of
Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed projects with
unreleased retention money and performance security posted in the
form of cash, in addition to the copies of said security/ Official Receipt
from the office of the BAC Chairman, which will be the basis in the
preparation of the Disbursement Voucher even without request for
payment from the contractors/supplier; and
c. Make a compilation of any record/documentation and prepare the
corresponding assessment/evaluation report as regards the long
outstanding Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees
accounts to support payment or for the possible reversion in order not
to overstate the liability accounts. Likewise, support the Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) with the Subsidiary Ledgers and all available
records in case of reversion to the unappropriated surplus, so that if
there may be claims in the future, the JEV would serve as the basis in
requesting for a supplemental budget for the settlement of the
payable.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the difference of ₱1,744,139.18 between the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and present the details to provide
the necessary adjusting/ correcting entries to bring the accurate balance of
the two reciprocal accounts for fair presentation of the same in the FS at
year-end.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit the amount of
₱1,478,315.44;
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer to see to it that collections are deposited
daily and intact with the authorized depository bank maintained by
the Municipality in line with PD No. 1445 to avoid the risk of
possible misappropriation/ misuse and/or loss of government funds;
c. Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
conduct monthly reconciliation of the cashbooks and ledgers,
respectively, thus both records are reconciled for any discrepancies.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to
furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of
the taxpayers or the RPTAR and the amount due and collectible for the
year, based on updated Assessment Roll, as basis of setting up RPT/SET
Receivable at the beginning of each year, pursuant to Section 20 of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE direct
the Municipal Accountant to establish the correct amounts of RPT and SET
Receivables accounts for fair presentation in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to
recompute the correct accumulated depreciation and prepare the necessary
adjusting journal entry by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and crediting
Accumulated Depreciation – Road Networks to restate the correct balance
of the PPE accounts in order to fairly present the FS.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF not
later than the fifth day of the ensuing month to ensure proper
monitoring of all accountable forms;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to
prepare and submit the monthly Pre-Closing Trial Balance and SAAO
not later that the 20th day after the end of the month, thus any
discrepancy will be communicated to management for its immediate
corrective action thus facilitate the preparation of complete year-end
FS and the determination of the status and balance of the
appropriations;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly
Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) in accordance with COA
Circular No. 96-011 to ensure the immediate reconciliation of the books
and the depository bank in order to have accurate cash in bank
balances at any given time.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide
a notice to the Audit Team within ten days, after the award of infrastructure
projects or before its start that the project signboard was already posted in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004, thus, enable the Auditor to
conduct timely verification and monitoring of the implemented
infrastructure projects, hence deficiencies, if any, could be communicated
to Management for its immediate and appropriate action. More so, the
constituents are also informed of such public concern, hence transparency
for good governance is ensured.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:
a. The GSO or the Inventory and Appraisal Committee, to submit the
required request for inspection of unserviceable properties supported
by the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties
(IIRUP), pictures, Sangguniang Bayan (SB) resolution for its disposal
and the Appraisal Report, if said properties were not yet disposed, for
its proper inspection.
b. If the unserviceable properties were already disposed, instruct the
disposal committee to submit a Narrative Report on the conducted
disposal;
c. The GSO, submit the letter request for authority to derecognize non-
existing/missing PPEs together with the required supporting
documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
a. BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to submit the final APP, including
amendments, revisions and supplemental, to the GPPB on or before the
end of January of the budget year;
b. BAC Chairman to make sure that the Technical Specifications for the
procurement of infrastructure projects and Technical Specifications
and Schedule of Requirements for the procurement of goods are duly
accomplished;
c. BAC Chairman to create a TWG, through a BAC resolution, and a
copy of which should be attached in the Disbursement Voucher or
submitted to the Audit Team;
d. BAC Secretariat to post the NOA in the PhilGEPS website within the
prescribed period of three calendar days in order to ensure
procurement transparency;
e. BAC Secretariat to see to it that all documents submitted by the
contractors, especially the Performance Security, are duly dated to
ensure that said security was posted prior to the signing of the contract;
f. BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG Members of the Municipality, at
all times, be guided with the IRR of RA No. 12009 or the New
Government Procurement Act in the procurement of goods and
services, and infrastructure projects to avoid the sanction provided
therein. Likewise, we encourage them to attend seminars related to the
procurement processes to keep them abreast of the latest updates and
issuance.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and
Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare the liquidation reports and
immediately remit the unutilized balances to the Bureau of Treasury or
return the unutilized funds to the source agencies to finally end any
obligation created by these transfers and to fully liquidate these accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer and the
MPDO and other concerned Department Heads to revisit relevant
documents of the programs/projects funded by NGAs and implement them
so that the purpose/s these funds were given will be achieved.

We also recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to determine the nature and
purpose/s of these fund transfers, date of the fund transfers, correct aging
of payables in order to facilitate the immediate implementation of the
projects and return of any unused funds to the source agencies.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and
MPDO to ensure that priority PPAs are in line with the duly approved
development plan of the LGU harmonized with the Regional Development
Plan and the Philippine Development Plan and not among those
expenditure items not allowed to be charged against the 20% DF in order
to avoid disallowance in audit and ensure full benefits towards its
constituents.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the
MDRRMO to prepare and submit respectively, the monthly Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit to the Office of the Auditor
to comply with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in order to facilitate timely
review, verification and monitoring of the utilization of the said fund.

We recommended the LCE instruct the MDRRMO to incorporate in the CY
2025 LDRRMFIP the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from previous
years to ensure the most possible available funds for disaster risk reduction.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the difference of P509,708.38 between the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and present the details to
provide correct and accurate balance for the two reciprocal accounts for
fair presentation of the same in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
committed to notify the creditors of their claims from the municipal
government which have been outstanding for two (2) years or more.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct further verification if
the obligated transactions were covered by perfected contracts,
otherwise those claims are considered not valid obligations and
cause the reversion of the Accounts Payable to maximize utilization
of public funds, to prevent possible accommodation of
undocumented Accounts Payable and to present fairly the financial
condition of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the:
3.1.6.1 Property and the Accounting Units to conduct collaborative
procedures in reconciling the difference noted in the balances of
the PPE account as presented in the Financial Statements with
the balances in the RPCPPE to support the accuracy and fair
presentation of the PPE account balances.
3.1.6.2 GSO to maintain an updated file of persons in actual possession
of the property to facilitate immediate monitoring of
Accountable persons and their liabilities in case of loss, damage,
or deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping and
use of property. Update the necessary information in the
RPCPPE to provide clear and complete information about the
actual condition, serviceability, property number, quantity,
correct value, Accountable Officer, and whereabouts of the
property.
3.1.6.3 GSO and the Municipal Accountant to apply the necessary
procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition
of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-
existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE
Account balances as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the General
Services Officer to dispose of the unserviceable properties as
prescribed in Section 356, Title VI, Book II of Local Government
Code and Section 79 of PD 1445. Also, follow strictly the rules and
regulations in the disposal of unserviceable properties specified in
COA Circular 92-386 to avoid further deterioration of the properties
and to avail of additional income that could be generated from the
sale of the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to classify the accounts based on the nature of the
transactions and use the prescribed account titles as set forth by COA
Circular No. 2015-009. Compliance to the provisions of the said circular
will result in a fair presentation of the related items in the financial
statements and provide correct and accurate information to the users of
the financial report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate and prioritize the
processing and transferring of the title of the land in the name of the
Municipality to support legal ownership of the land. Furthermore,
require the GSO Designate to indicate the necessary data in the RPCPPE
to provide brief description of the property for immediate reference in
the validation of the recorded Land in the Financial Statements. Also,
require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the relevant information pertaining to the Land account.

We recommended management to grant special cash advances only to
duly authorized/designated and properly bonded Disbursing Officers or
Accountable Officers thus, the risk that in the event of wrong usage of
the cash advances/shortages and losses, the Municipality has no
alternative from which to recover the funds. The Accountable Officer
shall prepare liquidation report with complete supporting documents and submit the same to the Accountant within the prescribed period as
required in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 thus,
enable the auditor to verify the accounts and any deficiency could be communicated to management for immediate and appropriate corrective actions thereof.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-382. Remit
the collections daily and deposit intact with the authorized depository
bank. Deposit on the next working day the balance of collections not
deposited during the day due to cut-off. Also, deposit immediately all the
long over-due undeposited collections to avoid possible misuse and
exposure of the funds to risk of loss or theft. Furthermore, we
recommend the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile their books to reflect the correct balance of Cash Local
Treasury Account.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of
accounts receivable and send notices to enforce collection of receivables
which are due and demandable to recover government funds and ensure
that loss or wastage of the same be prevented as provided in Section 2 of PD
1445. Also, management advice the municipal accountant to provide
impairment allowance when collection of the receivable is not possible
moreover, request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts where
settlement could no longer be ascertained as defined in COA Circular No.
2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management, require the Municipal Accountant
and GSO to follow the inventory accounting system/process including the
forms to be used and the reports to be prepared for the inventory to
present the actual status of Inventory of Supplies held for Consumption
and require the immediate issuance of the supplies to the suppose end-user
so that the latter would avail of the advantages from using the said
supplies. Also, avoid overstocking of perishable and time-sensitive goods to
prevent possible wastage of funds from maintaining expired or obsolete
products.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to immediately enforce the settlement of the audit suspensions and
require the other persons responsible to submit the necessary documents
as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009. A suspension shall be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from
receipt of the NS by the persons responsible to avoid possible maturing
of the said suspensions into disallowances.
